accept: Either: (a) MaxShortHexLen + idPattern accept up to 40 hex, AND envelope/payload validation continues to work, OR (b) spec is updated to say 'IDs on disk are always the short form, capped at 16 hex'. Pick one and make code + spec agree.
Whichever decision: a comment in ids.go cites the spec section that authoritatively states the cap.
Test covers IsValidID acceptance/rejection at the chosen boundary.
payload.description: Found by code reviewer 2026-05-10 at internal/ids/ids.go:103. var idPattern = regexp.MustCompile(`^act-[0-9a-f]{4,16}$`); MaxShortHexLen = 16 (ids.go:28). Spec describes full IDs as 40-hex sha256 digests used internally. IsValidID gates op.Envelope.Validate (envelope.go:77) and payload validation (payloads.go:90, 144, 161), so envelopes containing 17-40-hex ids would fail validation. Currently latent because no code path writes IDs longer than 16 hex, but becomes a real bug if the collision-extension protocol pushes an id past 16, or if the 'full 40-hex internally' intent is implemented.
payload.title: IsValidID caps at 16 hex; spec implies 40 hex internally
